title: AI Change And Governance
summary: Support sustainable AI adoption by planning for workflow change, decision rights, and simple governance that people can actually use.

lessons.sequenceRationale: This lesson exists here to move the learner from understanding the pattern to applying it on a live work artifact.
lessons.title: Why AI Initiatives Fail
lessons.subtitle: Failure usually comes from workflow gaps, trust gaps, and unclear ownership.
lessons.objective: Recognize the common causes of weak AI initiatives so you can avoid repeating them.
lessons.useWhen: Failure usually comes from workflow gaps, trust gaps, and unclear ownership.
lessons.whyItMatters: Many initiatives fail not because the model is weak, but because the surrounding workflow, training, and governance are unfinished.

coreConcepts.title: No Clear Workflow
coreConcepts.text: People are asked to "use AI more" without a defined process or use case.

coreConcepts.title: No Review Design
coreConcepts.text: Outputs are generated, but nobody knows what must be checked or approved.

coreConcepts.title: No Ownership
coreConcepts.text: Without clear ownership, the initiative becomes everybody’s idea and nobody’s responsibility.

workedExample.scenario: A team rolls out an AI tool broadly and sees little lasting use.
workedExample.approach: A review reveals there were no shared workflows, no training on review standards, and no owner for the rollout.
workedExample.result: The failure becomes understandable and fixable.
workedExample.verification: The team confirms the diagnosis with actual user feedback rather than guessing at the reasons.

doThisNow.task: Review one AI effort you have seen and note which failure pattern was present.
steps: Write one fix you would add.

commonMistakes: Calling adoption poor without asking why.

roleVariants.manager: Model safe adoption through small pilots, clear examples, and lightweight team habits.
roleVariants.writer: Document the prompts, examples, and edits that help others replicate quality.
roleVariants.analyst: Define simple measures and evidence standards so adoption conversations stay grounded.
roleVariants.operator: Focus on workflow change and training, not just tool access.

sourceNotes: Reviewed for business IC relevance and durable workflow guidance.
Keep verification and organizational policy checks explicit in future revisions.

lessons.sequenceRationale: This lesson exists here to move the learner from understanding the pattern to applying it on a live work artifact.
lessons.title: Use A Simple Transformation Sequence
lessons.subtitle: Discover, pilot, evaluate, and scale only what proves itself.
lessons.objective: Apply a lightweight sequence for moving from idea to tested AI workflow.
lessons.useWhen: Discover, pilot, evaluate, and scale only what proves itself.
lessons.whyItMatters: A small sequence keeps teams from leaping straight from curiosity to broad rollout.

coreConcepts.title: Discover
coreConcepts.text: Find the workflow and define the problem.

coreConcepts.title: Pilot
coreConcepts.text: Run a bounded test with a clear review path.

coreConcepts.title: Scale Carefully
coreConcepts.text: Expand only after the workflow shows evidence of value and manageable risk.

workedExample.scenario: A business unit wants to improve recurring report creation with AI.
workedExample.approach: They discover the main friction, pilot one report workflow, and measure the result before sharing broadly.
workedExample.result: The sequence produces evidence instead of hype.
workedExample.verification: They use the pilot evidence, not enthusiasm, to decide whether to scale.

doThisNow.task: Map one AI opportunity onto the simple transformation sequence.

commonMistakes: Treating the sequence as a bureaucratic exercise instead of a risk-control tool.

roleVariants.manager: Model safe adoption through small pilots, clear examples, and lightweight team habits.
roleVariants.writer: Document the prompts, examples, and edits that help others replicate quality.
roleVariants.analyst: Define simple measures and evidence standards so adoption conversations stay grounded.
roleVariants.operator: Focus on workflow change and training, not just tool access.

lessons.sequenceRationale: This lesson exists here to move the learner from understanding the pattern to applying it on a live work artifact.
lessons.title: Use Lightweight Governance
lessons.subtitle: Governance should clarify what people can do, not freeze the work.
lessons.objective: Define simple governance elements that keep AI use safe and practical.
lessons.useWhen: Governance should clarify what people can do, not freeze the work.
lessons.whyItMatters: Overly heavy governance kills momentum, but no governance creates avoidable risk and mistrust.

coreConcepts.title: Clarify Approved Paths
coreConcepts.text: People need to know which tools, data classes, and workflows are allowed.

coreConcepts.title: Clarify Review And Escalation
coreConcepts.text: The governance needs a clear answer to "who checks this?" and "who decides if this is okay?"

coreConcepts.title: Clarify Ownership
coreConcepts.text: Someone should own the workflow, the training, and the evidence collection.

workedExample.scenario: A team is interested in AI but avoids it because the rules feel unclear.
workedExample.approach: A lead creates a simple approved-path guide with tool, data, review, and escalation rules.
workedExample.result: People start using AI more confidently within the boundary.
workedExample.verification: They check whether the guide answered the real questions users were asking.

doThisNow.task: Write the minimum governance notes needed for one AI workflow you want to support.
steps: Name the reviewer and escalation path.

commonMistakes: Creating policy with no workflow connection.
Making the rules so vague that nobody can use them.

roleVariants.writer: Document the prompts, examples, and edits that help others replicate quality.
roleVariants.analyst: Define simple measures and evidence standards so adoption conversations stay grounded.
roleVariants.operator: Focus on workflow change and training, not just tool access.

lessons.sequenceRationale: This lesson exists here to move the learner from understanding the pattern to applying it on a live work artifact.
lessons.title: Support The Human Change
lessons.subtitle: People need habits, examples, and confidence, not just access.
lessons.objective: Plan for the human side of AI-enabled workflow change.
lessons.useWhen: People need habits, examples, and confidence, not just access.
lessons.whyItMatters: Adoption fails when teams treat AI as a tool installation instead of a workflow change.

coreConcepts.title: Change The Workflow, Not Just The Tool
coreConcepts.text: People need to know how their daily work should change.

coreConcepts.title: Provide Examples
coreConcepts.text: Examples lower cognitive load and increase trust.

coreConcepts.title: Normalize Review
coreConcepts.text: The team should see review and correction as part of the process, not as failure.

workedExample.scenario: A rollout gives everyone access to AI but usage remains low.
workedExample.approach: The team adds workflow examples, office hours, and a review checklist.
workedExample.result: Usage improves because the change becomes concrete and supported.
workedExample.verification: They ask whether people can now describe exactly when and how to use AI in one recurring task.

doThisNow.task: Choose one support element your team is currently missing for AI adoption.
steps: Write how you would add it this month.

commonMistakes: Failing to show the changed workflow.

roleVariants.manager: Model safe adoption through small pilots, clear examples, and lightweight team habits.
roleVariants.writer: Document the prompts, examples, and edits that help others replicate quality.
roleVariants.analyst: Define simple measures and evidence standards so adoption conversations stay grounded.
roleVariants.operator: Focus on workflow change and training, not just tool access.

sourceNotes: Reviewed for business IC relevance and durable workflow guidance.
Keep verification and organizational policy checks explicit in future revisions.

lessons.sequenceRationale: This lesson exists here to move the learner from understanding the pattern to applying it on a live work artifact.
lessons.title: Write A 90-Day Action Plan
lessons.subtitle: A short plan helps move from intention to measured change.
lessons.objective: Create a 90-day plan for one AI workflow or team adoption effort.
lessons.useWhen: A short plan helps move from intention to measured change.
lessons.whyItMatters: A 90-day horizon is long enough to learn something and short enough to stay concrete.

coreConcepts.title: Pick One Priority Workflow
coreConcepts.text: Trying to transform everything at once creates noise.

coreConcepts.title: Define Milestones
coreConcepts.text: Discovery, pilot, review, and next-step decisions should all be visible.

coreConcepts.title: Assign Evidence
coreConcepts.text: Decide what examples or metrics will show progress.

workedExample.scenario: A team wants to improve recurring internal reporting with AI.
workedExample.approach: They build a 90-day plan covering design, pilot, evidence review, and team sharing.
workedExample.result: The work becomes easier to coordinate and less likely to stall.
workedExample.verification: They check whether each milestone names an owner and a specific output.

doThisNow.task: Draft a 90-day plan for one AI initiative you can actually influence.
steps: Set the evidence and review date.

commonMistakes: Writing an initiative with no clear owner.
Trying to cover too many workflows.

roleVariants.analyst: Define simple measures and evidence standards so adoption conversations stay grounded.
roleVariants.operator: Focus on workflow change and training, not just tool access.

outcomes: Use lightweight governance that people can actually follow.
Support human workflow change, not just tool rollout.
nextModuleBridge: Next, measure what changed so AI improvement becomes credible, not anecdotal.
